Provider cycles report the chain, not a misleading missing-provider error.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- web/web.go --
package web

import "net/http"

type A struct{}
type B struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewA(b *B) *A { return &A{} }

//fabrik:provider
func NewB(a *A) *B { return &B{} }

type Handlers struct {
	A *A
}

//fabrik:http GET /
func (h *Handlers) Index(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/diags --
error: web/web.go:12:11: provider cycle: *web.A -> *web.B -> *web.A
  help: break the cycle by removing one of the dependencies
